title: WfOS Level 0 namespace migration (krakenâ†’cthulhu, hypercubeâ†’polytope, dustâ†’panoply, etherâ†’wisp, archonâ†’ontarch)
changed_paths: Build/src/workspaces/wfos/docs/*.md (19 docs, content sweep + semantic fixes for cth portable/meta/native/tendril commands)
Build/src/workspaces/wfos/packages/ontarch/registry/sessions/*.json (30+ historical records, content rewritten to new names)
Workstreams/.agents/{README, profiles/*.toml, profiles/README, graphs/README, tools/README, skills/README}
Plan/bin/wfos/lg_wfos_prod_mindflow.md â†’ Plan/bin/archives/lg_wfos_prod_mindflow.md (archived; brand retired per Â§18)
Plan/bin/{wfos/*, toolkit/*, workstreams/*, root docs}.md (content sweep + mindflow retirement notes)
commands_run: git mv (package dirs + bin scripts + docs + spec files, in nested wfos repo and root repo)
perl -pi -e (bulk content sweep across tracked files in wfos repo, .agents, Build/bin, Plan/bin)
moon run ontarch:secrets-scan (detect mode â€” pre-existing historical finding, not introduced by this migration)
outcome: Applied the Level 0 namespace alignment v0.2 everywhere. Package dirs renamed in the nested wfos repo via git mv: dustâ†’panoply, archonâ†’ontarch, krakenâ†’cthulhu, hypercubeâ†’polytope, etherâ†’wisp. Renamed all bin scripts (panoply-*, ontarch-*), config/shell/panoply.zsh, manifest/panoply.tools.toml, descriptors/policies/schemas (dustâ†’panoply). Env vars swept DUST_*â†’PANOPLY_*, ARCHON_*â†’ONTARCH_*. Moon project ids + tasks renamed (panoply:*, ontarch:*). Archetype IDs updated native-substrateâ†’native-toolchain, portable-runtimeâ†’portable-component-runtime. All 19 wfos docs rewritten with alignment Â§4/Â§9 semantics: cth portable/native/meta/tendril/package command surface, integrations/ namespace inside cthulhu (no tendril package), Tendril brand vocabulary. All 30+ historical session JSONs in ontarch/registry/sessions/ had content rewritten to new names (filenames preserved). Workstreams root AGENTS.md + .agents/{profiles,READMEs,graphs,tools} swept to new policy paths (packages/ontarch/policies/panoply.agent.policy.toml) and PANOPLY_AGENT env flag. Build/bin specs renamed (E03_native_toolchain, H09_runtime_controller_cthulhu, H11_portable_component_runtime_translator, H12_workflow_automation, 2026-06-23-epic-3-native-toolchain-panoply-sprint) and all content swept; H12 Mindflow brand stripped (retired per Â§18), Casper noted as conceptual/planned per Â§17. Plan/bin renames (lg_wfos_lvl_0_native_toolchain, lg_wfos_lvl_0_portable_component_runtime) and lg_wfos_prod_mindflow archived with retirement note; all Plan/bin content swept with archetype-first wording at Plan level. Mis-sweep fixed: external 'dust' du-CLI references in panoply manifest + tool-catalog + native-toolchain doc + E03 sprint restored to 'external dust' (was wrongly swept to 'panoply').
validation: wfos repo gates after rename: panoply:doctor PASS (44 tools, secrets rail ENFORCED under PANOPLY_AGENT=1); panoply:gen-check PASS; panoply:validate-substrate PASS; panoply:validate-dotfiles PASS; panoply:validate-secrets PASS; ontarch:sync OK (4 policies, local-toolkit.yml 44 tools); ontarch:scan OK; ontarch:validate PASS (graph 28 nodes / 27 edges â€” all endpoints resolve, restored to pre-migration count after .agents profile rails swept to panoply.agent); ontarch:bin-report PASS. ontarch:secrets-scan detect mode FAILS on a pre-existing historical finding (age-secret-key in packages/dust/secrets/README.md at commit a3a4978, 2026-06-24; the key was redacted in HEAD but persists in git history) â€” NOT introduced by this migration; staged pre-commit hook (gitleaks git --staged) unaffected. Final rg sweep: no stray kraken|hypercube|dust|ether|archon|native-substrate|portable-runtime|krk|hqb|DUST_|ARCHON_ references remain outside the alignment doc's own alias/history tables, Plan/bin/archives/, and legitimate English words (together/whether/SoftEther).
decisions: Full rename (option a) per user approval: git mv package dirs + moon project ids + env vars + scripts + policies + descriptors, not just docs.
Historical session JSONs rewritten to new names (filenames preserved) per user approval â€” alias tables in the alignment doc carry the migration record instead.

Mindflow brand fully retired per alignment Â§18: H12 renamed to H12_workflow_automation, lg_wfos_prod_mindflow.md archived with header note, process described as 'planning-capture' / 'captureâ†’specâ†’promote loop' going forward.
Casper kept as conceptual/planned per alignment Â§17 â€” all refs already say 'planned'; no rename needed.
Tendril is Cthulhu-internal vocabulary only â€” no packages/tendril/ dir created; cthulhu README + runtime-controller doc note the integrations/ namespace and Tendril brand relationship.

External 'dust' du-CLI references (panoply manifest, tool-catalog, native-toolchain doc, E03 sprint) manually restored after the mechanical sweep wrongly renamed them to 'panoply' â€” the clash note now reads 'chosen over the external dust CLI, which clashed with the former native-toolchain brand'.
Session id uses a descriptive form (2026-07-18-namespace-migration) rather than eNN-sN since this is cross-cutting maintenance, not a numbered epic story.
next: H09 â€” draft sprint/spec (horizon epic; not session-ready)
blockers: ontarch:secrets-scan detect mode reports a pre-existing age-secret-key in git history (commit a3a4978, packages/dust/secrets/README.md line 31, 2026-06-24). The key is redacted in HEAD but persists in the historical blob. Not introduced by this migration. Resolution requires a human decision: history rewrite, .gitleaksignore fingerprint entry, or accept as historical test fixture. Pre-commit staged hook is unaffected.
token_note: One cross-cutting migration session vs N per-namespace sessions â€” the alignment doc is the single source of truth, and a coordinated sweep + git mv + single validation pass is cheaper than re-reading context for 5 separate renames. Trade-off: larger blast radius per session, mitigated by running the renamed gates immediately after the sweep.
